title: Xtra Juicy French Chicken Gratin
description: A nutty and juicy French dish featuring pecorino and score-fat, perfect for a sick day, and paleo-friendly. This recipe is a twist on traditional French cuisine with a focus on healthy ingredients.
instructions: Season the chicken thighs with salt and pepper.
In a large skillet, heat the score-fat over medium heat and sear the chicken thighs until browned.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
Add the sliced onion to the skillet and cook until softened.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown.
In a large baking dish, arrange the cooked onion and mushroom mixture in the bottom of the dish.
Place the browned chicken thighs on top of the onion and mushroom mixture.
Sprinkle the shaved pecorino cheese over the top of the chicken.
Pour the chicken broth over the top of the cheese and sprinkle with thyme.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
